phase: 8 of 9 complete. Phase 9 (updated §9 test contract) is the FINAL phase
nextAgent: Phase 9: the §9 quality contract — verify the required property/invariant suites all exist and pass (minimalism, goal-driven, period safety, SPF mandate, dedup, adaptation regression, explainability invariant |shelf|=|routine|+|frozen|+|reserve|, determinism), enforce no Math.random / no unsorted object iteration in the engine, and reconcile the roadmap/final state. Most suites already exist from phases 1-8 — Phase 9 is largely verification + gap-filling + the explainability invariant test.

artifacts.qaTests: — (Phase 1 is pure data/logic; unit tests co-located in src/utils/routineEngine/ per .claude/rules/testing.md)

baseline.result: full jest --testPathIgnorePatterns=worktrees -> 1218 passed / 3 known pre-existing failed / 2 todo
baseline.fullRun: npx jest src tests -> 1089 passed / 3 failed; the 3 are PRE-EXISTING (verified by stashing Phase 1 and re-running clean HEAD, which fails identically): tests/catalog/catalog-screen (AsyncStorage native module null), tests/catalog/product-detail (palette.cobaltTint undefined), tests/shelf-filtering/PaoChip.
baseline.note: Phase 2+ must not reduce this. Changed expectations must be justified in the progress log per architecture-review.md §6.

process.mode: inline — all four roles, no subagents (user directive 2026-07-17, applies to phases 1-9)
process.review: tech-lead replaced by a structured self-review pass, run SEPARATELY after implementation, judged strictly against artifacts (corrected phase file, discrepancy report, .claude/rules/architecture-review.md, the diff). A decision not written down is documented or flagged as a deviation. Output = pass/fail checklist with file/line refs; any FAIL is fixed and the checklist re-run.
reviewChecklist: single conflict matrix (pairRules), old conflictRulesDb collapsed
isStrongActive <=> irritancy >= 3 invariant, no exemption lists
cumulative active cap + rinseOff exemption implemented as specified
migrations idempotent (double-run test exists and passes)
every acceptance criterion in the corrected phase file mapped to a passing test
npx tsc --noEmit clean; no Math.random / unsorted object iteration in engine code

handoffNotes: PHASE 8 COMPLETE (commit 66f4129). Schema is now CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION=3 (ONE bump). New: peptide re-attribution (copper_peptides tag w/o copper INCI -> peptide_signal, in migrateProductActiveKeys), phototypeNeedsConfirmation flag + PhototypeConfirmBanner. Vitamin C prompt (8.2) and goal derivation (8.4) were ALREADY shipped — verified, not re-implemented. Idempotent + same-reference contract held.

PHASE 9 is the LAST phase and is mostly VERIFICATION. Required §9 suites and where they live: minimalism/goal-driven/dedup/cumulative (skeleton.test.ts + generate.test.ts phase-04 block); period safety (period-safety.test.ts); SPF mandate (mandates.test.ts + seasonal-masks.test.ts); adaptation regression (adaptation.test.ts + cycling-and-adaptation.test.ts); determinism + source-invariance (determinism-and-safety.test.ts). GAP to add: the explainability invariant |shelf| = |routine| + |frozen| + |reserve| as a property test, and a no-Math.random/no-unsorted-object-iteration guard over the engine (grep-based test).

PHASE 7 COMPLETE (commit 5d4c08a). DecisionReasonCode is a CLOSED union in src/constants/decisionReasons.ts (46 codes, satisfies-guarded dictionary). NO template-literal member (Phase 4 killed stacking_cap_*). reasonCode is now DECOUPLED from ruleId everywhere: pairRules carry reasonCode, resolve/validate emit the code + keep ruleId. Integrity test forbids rule_* in any reasonCode (both directions). If you add a new engine reasonCode literal, it MUST be added to the enum or rulesetIntegrity fails.

OVERRIDE flow: EngineInput.userOverrides (product ids) -> selectSkeleton rescues reserved products into eligible periods only (retinoid-in-AM impossible by construction). Persistence in trackingStore.overrides + overrideHash; routinePlanActions.computeOverrideHash / activeOverrides gate on shelf+goal hash. generatePlan stays pure. DEVIATION: override lives in trackingStore not routinesStore (same home as firstScheduledDates — user->engine runtime inputs).

Phase 8 REMINDER: schema is still at CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION=2. Phase 8 bumps to 3. Goal derivation ALREADY runs in migrateProfile (phase-03) — Phase 8 only re-persists + bumps. Remaining Phase 8 work: peptide re-attribution migration, phototype 6-card confirmation prompt UI, vitamin C wizard prompt UI (vitaminCAutoMigrated flag exists). All idempotent + same-reference on second run (migrations.ts:16 contract).

PHASE 6 COMPLETE (commit eae8ba6). Dynamic cycle phase resolves against shelf composition: resolveCyclePhase(state, available:Set<string>) in cycleState.ts degrades exf/retinoid night to recovery when the class is absent, WITHOUT touching cyclePhaseIndex (checkInCycle untouched). availableCycleClasses (dailyView) counts only eligible+non-frozen products. getDynamicCycleStatus feeds the Today card. NEW reason code: dynamic_unavailable_no_actives (Phase 7 enum must include it).

PHASE 6 STALE PREMISES (both verified false, no machinery built): §6.2 dynamic never discards scheduledDays (render-gating; round-trip test proves it). §6.4 seasonMask already pure (source-invariance pin added). Same pattern as earlier stale premises — verify before implementing.

REASON CODES accumulated for Phase 7 enum (from phases 3-6): goal_exclude, barrier_repair_excludes_irritants, spf_required_goal (P3); reserve/info actions + not_needed_for_goals, duplicate_function, cumulative_active_cap, moisturizer_recommended, rinse_off_active_note, exfoliant_treatment_cap, reclassified_treatment_cap (P4); dynamic_unavailable_no_actives (P6). PLUS the 22 pre-existing JSON codes + code-level (product_hidden, pao_expired, no_allowed_period, relocation_rejected). reasonCode STILL doubles as ruleId in ladder freezes (resolve.ts:410) — Phase 7 must split them (§4.4 ruling: enum stays decoupled from pair-rule IDs; add reasonCode to pairRules).

PHASE 5 COMPLETE (commit b617da3). Adaptation anchored on firstScheduledDate (trackingStore.firstScheduledDates, written on SAVE via applyRoutinePlan — engine stays pure). REVERSED the V2 "no retroactive throttling" decision: never-scheduled product -> phase 1 (JSDoc updated + logged). Phase regression (applyAdaptationRegression) throttles irritancy>=3 products after 14/28-day breaks. Tolerability term now LIVE in scoreCandidate (was 0 in Phase 4): phaseIndex/2 * 200, adapted beats new for a contested treatment slot.

INTEGRATION FIX shipped in Phase 5: buildEngineInputFromStores (routinePlanActions) now threads primaryGoal/secondaryGoal + firstScheduledDates. Before this the LIVE app generated maintenance plans regardless of the users goal — a latent Phase 3 gap that Phase 4 made visible. If you touch that function, keep the goals threaded.
ProductApplicationStats gained firstAppliedDate (null for pre-phase-5 stats). Any fixture building a stats object must include it. TrackingInput.firstScheduledDates is optional (defaults {}).

PHASE 4 COMPLETE (commit c0afcb4) — the core rewrite. Skeleton build-up (src/utils/routineEngine/skeleton.ts) replaced greedy admission: selectSkeleton runs BEFORE resolvePeriods and hands it periodCandidates + treatmentCaps. resolve.ts still owns the ladder/relocation/slot-alternatives unchanged; buildPools just intersects with the selection. A goal-less (maintenance) profile now RESERVES every active — any test pushing an active through generatePlan must pass a matching goal.

CUMULATIVE CAP is live: findCapViolations (resolve.ts) = one strong leave-on carrier (irritancy>=3 && !rinseOff) per period per DAY; per-class stacking is GONE from actives.json. Two strong actives same-day is now cumulative_active_cap (avoid) in BOTH generation (skeleton reserves the loser) and validate (user-saved routines). Pair escalation between two strong carriers is redundant — the cap already forbids them; escalation only still matters for a strong+mild caution pair (e.g. vitC_pure + copper_peptides).

SCORING for Phase 5: scoreCandidate has a tolerability term at weight 200 currently fed 0. Phase 5 feeds it phaseIndex-derived 0|100|200 (0-based phaseIndex/2*200 → 0|100|200). goalRank*1000 dominates it, tolerability breaks ties among same-rank candidates — the intended "adapted product outranks a new one of the same class".

Phase 5 CONSTRAINT (unchanged): generatePlan is pure and never writes. firstScheduledDate must be recorded on the save path (src/domain/routinePlanActions.ts) and threaded back via TrackingInput. Phase 5 also REVERSES the documented "no retroactive throttling" decision in adaptation.ts:31-35 — update that JSDoc and log the reversal or tech-lead auto-blocks.

Reason codes introduced in Phase 4 (Phase 7 enum must cover): reserve, info, goal_exclude (actions); cumulative_active_cap, not_needed_for_goals, duplicate_function, moisturizer_recommended, rinse_off_active_note (reason codes). Treatment cap codes: exfoliant_treatment_cap, reclassified_treatment_cap. reasonCode still doubles as ruleId in ladder freezes — Phase 7 splits them.

PHASE 3 COMPLETE (commit 1406475). Step 0 exists: RoutineContext carries goals + treatmentClassRanking + goalDecisions; generatePlan prepends Step-0 decisions. SCORING IS UNTOUCHED — Phase 4 wires the ranking into scoreCandidate (goalRank*1000 term) and the skeleton fill. EngineInput.profile goal fields are optional (absent = maintenance) so all fixtures compile.

Attribution machinery for Phase 4+: ProductFacts classes now resolve through position-gated parsing — glycerin_class needs position <= 5; aha/bha/vitamin_c_pure past position 8 carry potency low (trace). Wizard tags are never gated. parseActiveIngredientDetails.position is the single source of position data.

Phase 8 note: §8.4 goal derivation is ALREADY LIVE via migrateProfile (idempotent, every hydrate). Phase 8 executes only the schema bump 2->3 + one-time re-persist; do not re-implement the derivation.
Consultant review list (3 items, values not structure): goals rankings in actives.json; glycerin requireWithinPosition=5; trace downgradeToLowAfterPosition=8 + downgrade-vs-drop policy.

PHASE 2 COMPLETE (commit d96c5b6; Phase 1 = dfa61f6). §4.2 RESOLVED: pm_preferred dropped, AHA/BHA hard pm + PHA both, locked by rulesetIntegrity "PM-only eligibility lock". §4.4 RESOLVED: enum decoupled from pair-rule IDs (ruleId = optional payload only). Base mandates block now exists in actives.json (RulesetMandate type); collectRequireMandates has FOUR sources — clinical, seasonal, phototype, base. Phase 3 spf_required_goal should be a goal-conditioned entry alongside spf_photosensitizing, NOT a new mechanism.

Period-safety property suite: tests/routine-engine/period-safety.test.ts. Any future phase touching allowedPeriods aggregation, relocation, or dailyView must keep it green — it is the §4.2 ruling in executable form.

PHASE 1 IS COMPLETE AND SELF-REVIEWED (ACCEPT), uncommitted on engine-improvements. Shipped: irritancy recalibration + irritancyByPotency on retinoid; resolveIrritancy/isStrongActive in rulesetTypes.ts as the single mild/strong boundary; peptide_signal + peptide_neuro with a conservative unknown-peptide fallback; conflict matrix consolidated onto actives.json pairRules (INGREDIENT_CONFLICT_RULES deleted, rule_vitc_niacinamide retired, 2 rules added); rinseOff on ProductFacts; ruleset version -> 2026-07-17.

PHASE 1 DEVIATION CARRIED INTO PHASE 3: glycerin_class was NOT added. Glycerin/glycols are in most formulations, so attributing by bare INCI presence tagged nearly every product and would flatten Phase 3 goal scoring (every product would hit the dryness concern). It has no Phase 1 consumer. Phase 3 adds it WITH the goals block and must pick a real attribution rule — INCI position (lists are concentration-ordered) or productType, not bare presence. This is a clinical-consultant data question, same review as the goals values.

PHASE 1 DEVIATION ALREADY APPLIED (was scheduled for Phase 4): pha lost its stacking block AND left aha/bha sharedCapWith. The Phase 1 invariant (stacking iff irritancy>=3) fails on pha at irritancy 1. Both edits are inseparable: leaving pha in the shared group makes the cap admission-order dependent (PHA-first blocks a later AHA; AHA-first does not) — a determinism defect. AHA + PHA in one PM is now legal, as report §7.2 predicted; exfoliant exposure is still capped by phototype_pih_exfoliant_cap and summer_uv_exfoliant_limit.

PHASE 4 REMINDER: assumption 8.1 still holds — the cumulative cap subsumes per-class stacking, so Phase 4 deletes the remaining stacking blocks (including the vitamin_c_pure one Phase 1 just added, which is the short-lived churn 8.1 predicted) and restates the invariant as "subject to the cumulative cap iff irritancy >= 3". rinseOff is already shipped and waiting for its Phase 4 consumer.

PHASE 7 REMINDER (sharpens §4.4): reasonCode and ruleId are conflated at resolve.ts:410 (`reasonCode: primary.ruleId`), so FrozenItem.reasonCode currently holds pair-rule IDs. Phase 1 changed that id set (rule_vitc_niacinamide deleted, 2 added), which is exactly why the enum must not absorb rule IDs — add a reasonCode field to pairRules and split the two vocabularies first.

New shared primitive: conflictEngine.ts exports matchPairRule(keyA, keyB) — the ONE place a class pair is matched against pairRules. src/utils/productForm/conflictPreview.ts (a third legacy-table consumer the tech design missed) now calls it too, so the preview and the real check cannot disagree. Reuse it rather than re-implementing side matching; pairRules sides may be arrays.

READ DISCREPANCY-REPORT.md BEFORE ANY PHASE. The V2.1 package was authored against the V2 design document, not the merged V2 implementation. 4 of 9 phases rested on problems that do not exist. A session that trusts the original prose will 'fix' non-bugs and fork the taxonomy.

Already exist — do not build: the irritancy scale (actives.json properties.irritancy, all 15 classes); PERIOD_ELIGIBILITY (= allowedPeriods + preferredPeriod, matches the spec 1:1); the Path A period guard (resolve.ts:420 — retinoid-in-AM already unreachable); the planContainsProperty:photosensitizing SPF mechanism; a pure seasonMask (engine takes it as EngineInput); the phototype + vitamin C migrations (schema v2); recency-free scoring.
Do not exist despite the spec naming them: PEPT tag, maxActivesPerPeriod, skinIssues (it is concerns: SkinConcern[]), assets/inci_seed.json, InciRepository, anything pregnancy-related.

src/constants/rulesets/actives.json is the single source of truth. activeRegistry.ts and conflictMatrix.ts are NOT created — folded in. A third conflict table is a tech-lead BLOCKER under architecture-review.md §3.

Conventions that override the package's prose: class keys keep persisted names (copper_peptides, ceramides, cica, hyaluronic_acid, spf_filters — never peptide_copper); severity is avoid/caution (never high/medium); reason codes are lower_snake_case (never SCREAMING_CASE).

PHASE 1 SCOPE: irritancy recalibration (bha 2->3, vitamin_c_pure 2->3, benzoyl_peroxide 3->4, azelaic_acid 1->2, copper_peptides 1->2); irritancyByPotency on retinoid (low/medium 3, high/rx 4) resolved in productFacts.aggregateProperties; isStrongActive = irritancy >= 3 derivation + rulesetIntegrity invariant (stacking declared iff strong -> vitamin_c_pure gains stacking); peptide_signal / peptide_neuro / glycerin_class classes + matchers + the contains-'peptide' fallback to peptide_signal; pairRules consolidation (delete conflictRulesDb INGREDIENT_CONFLICT_RULES, repoint ConflictEngine, remove rule_vitc_niacinamide, add vitc_pure+copper_peptides and vitc_derivative+bpo); rinseOff on ProductFacts.

PHASE 1 NON-SCOPE: the cumulative cap is ENFORCED in Phase 4, not here. rinseOff is unused until Phase 4 — that is intended, not dead code. No cumulative_active_cap emission in Phase 1. pregnancyBlocked class property is NOT added (deferred with report §4.3).
OPEN QUESTION §4.1 RESOLVED (user, 2026-07-17): vitamin_c_pure -> irritancy 3, cap intended. Superseded in scope by the cumulative active exposure rule (report §7).

OPEN QUESTIONS STILL BLOCKING LATER PHASES: §4.2 pm_preferred for AHA/BHA loosens a shipped PM-only rule (blocks Phase 2; recommend dropping); §4.3 pregnancy is a whole subsystem not a flag (blocks Phase 3; recommend splitting out); §4.4 the closed DecisionReasonCode enum must absorb 22 existing JSON codes + a synthesized stacking_cap_* family, AND resolve.ts:410 writes reasonCode: primary.ruleId so pair-rule IDs leak into the reason vocabulary — the enum cannot close until reasonCode and ruleId are split (blocks Phase 7).

CUMULATIVE ACTIVE EXPOSURE RULE (user directive 2026-07-17, report §7) amends Phases 1 and 4. An active class is a property of a product, not a slot. Mild (irritancy <= 2): no cumulative restriction, any number, any slot; class-dedup applies ONLY to treatment-candidate selection. Strong (irritancy >= 3) leave-on: one carrier per period across ALL slots; reclassified as a treatment candidate in any format (acid toner/cream = treatments), inheriting treatment frequency caps and never a structural slot's daily frequency; the vacated structural slot takes a neutral alternative or renders a placeholder. Rinse-off carriers are exempt with an info-level note. Reason code cumulative_active_cap.

Four sub-questions the directive does not state, resolved as DOCUMENTED ASSUMPTIONS (report §7.1-§7.4), not silently: (8.1) the cumulative cap subsumes per-class stacking — Phase 1 adds stacking to vitamin_c_pure to keep the shipped invariant coherent, Phase 4 deletes all stacking blocks; deliberate short-lived churn. (8.2) AHA + PHA in one period becomes LEGAL — PHA is mild so it leaves aha.sharedCapWith; a loosening required by the mild/strong split; if unintended, PHA needs irritancy 3 or an explicit exfoliant-family cap. (8.3) 'treatment frequency caps' resolve per-class — exfoliant cap for exfoliants, existing adaptation phase caps for retinoids; no new table; enforced invariant is the narrow 'never daily'. (8.4) rinseOff true for cleanser + makeup_remover only; peeling and mask are conservatively leave-on since peel gels rinse but pads do not and sleeping masks are leave-on.

PHASE 5 CARRIES A DOCUMENTED-DECISION REVERSAL: adaptation.ts:31-35 states 'A product owned long before tracking shipped lands directly in phase 3 (no retroactive throttling)' as deliberate design. The usage-anchor intent requires the opposite. The implementer must update that JSDoc and log the reversal, or architecture-review.md §6 auto-blocks.

PHASE 5 CONSTRAINT: generatePlan is pure and never writes (generate.ts:39). firstScheduledDate must be recorded on the save path (src/domain/routinePlanActions.ts) and threaded back in via TrackingInput — do not make the engine write.
Phase 8 depends on 1 AND 3 (it executes the goal migration). Only ONE schema bump for the whole package: CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION 2 -> 3.
